title: Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles
caption:
Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles, 2023

Measles is a highly contagious viral disease, most common in young children. Its effects include blindness, inflammation of the brain, severe diarrhea, and severe respiratory infections such as pneumonia.

This choropleth map displays the absolute number of one-year-olds who missed measles vaccination in 2023, using a continuous color scale ranging from zero to one million. The visualization highlights a heavy concentration of unvaccinated children in specific populous nations, with the darkest red shades indicating the highest burden. India and Nigeria appear in the deepest red, placing them in the highest category with over 1 million unvaccinated one-year-olds each. Pakistan and the Democratic Republic of the Congo also show very high numbers, falling within the 300,000 to 1 million band. Across Asia, China and Indonesia display significant gaps in coverage, with figures between 100,000 and 300,000. In the Americas, Mexico and Brazil show moderate numbers in the 30,000 to 100,000 range, while the United States and Canada fall into lower bands. Most of Western Europe, Australia, and parts of South America show very light shading, indicating fewer than 10,000 unvaccinated children. Greenland and several island nations are hatched to indicate no available data. The distribution shows that the majority of unvaccinated children globally are clustered in a small number of countries in South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa.
